archive_check: No same-industry entries in designs/archive.json (archive is empty)
anti_patterns: 3-column card grid with icon + title + 2-line description (the services grid cliche)
Gradient overlay on hero stock photo of wood/tools
Green colour scheme (the generic trade/eco default)
Pill-shaped skill/service tags in a horizontal wrap
Full-width coloured CTA banner between sections
Trust badges strip with checkmark icons (5-Star Rated, Fully Insured, etc.)
Stock photo of a person in a hard hat or holding a tape measure
Hamburger menu sliding in from the right with full-screen dark overlay
Rustic/farmhouse aesthetic with wood-grain textures as backgrounds
Carousel/slider testimonials that auto-rotate (Grovewood does this — noted as weak execution by Akin)
Generic hero headline: 'Welcome to [Business Name]' or 'Your Local Trusted Joiner'
Google Maps embed as a full-width section (lazy, takes up space, rarely clicked)
design_constraints_from_assessment: Design must look premium with basic phone photography or placeholder images
Must include a How We Work process section that builds trust without testimonials
Gallery must look intentional even with only 3-4 images or placeholders
No heritage messaging — owner is 26, lead with quality and ambition
Services must clearly distinguish house building (premium) from general carpentry

themes.name: Forge
themes.personality: Dark, confident, and warm — a modern workshop feel where the craftsmanship speaks through structure and type, not stock photography
themes.inspired_by: Grovewood Joinery — the gold-on-dark palette and How We Work section. But with better typography and no carousel testimonials.
themes.signature_element: A 4-step numbered process section (Discuss → Design → Build → Deliver) with large serif numbers and connecting horizontal lines on dark background — the centrepiece trust-builder

themes.name: Grain
themes.personality: Light, airy, and tactile — like a freshly planed piece of timber. Warm whites and a single amber accent create space for the work to breathe
themes.inspired_by: The whitespace of bilal.world and the editorial warmth of Thomas May, but on a light background. Proves you don't need dark mode to feel premium.
themes.signature_element: Services presented as large editorial text blocks with amber underlines — not cards, not icons, just beautiful typography with bold service names and flowing descriptions beneath

themes.name: Workshop
themes.personality: Honest, modern, and grounded — the digital equivalent of stepping into a clean, well-organised joiner's workshop
themes.inspired_by: SPLINTR's portfolio confidence and James Smith Furniture's artisan authenticity, but adapted for a local trade context rather than a London design studio.
themes.signature_element: Gallery as a 2-column masonry layout where each image has a project title overlay that fades in on hover — looks intentional even with 3-4 images because the layout is designed for irregular counts, not locked to multiples of 3
